mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth expands corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in GDP expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the federal funds rate raise discount rates and signal aggressive policy tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS rising > 0.25pp in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the unemployment rate and declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate labor market deterioration, signaling impending economic contraction and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls concurrently over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts combined with falling residential investment reflects weakening consumer demand and credit conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite falling HOUST and RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing and industrial activity, supporting corporate revenue growth and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in industrial expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when monthly CPI inflation exceeds 0.5% over a 1-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant real GDP growth coupled with rising consumer prices indicates stagflation, which compresses equity multiples due to margin pressure and higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline when GDPC1 growth is flat or negative while CPIAUCSL rises over a 1-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in total nonfarm payrolls signals rapid labor market deterioration and impending recession, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window despite PAYEMS drops > 100k in a single month.
